# UC-22.40.5 · High-risk privileged-session command without JIT approval

## Description

Correlates privileged-session command execution against a catalogue of destructive, mass-data, or security-control commands and requires a matching JIT approval within the prior hour. Missing approvals are treated as a finding.

## Value

Makes 'we require JIT approval for dangerous commands' an observable, always-on control.

## Implementation

(1) Curate high_risk_commands.csv with regex patterns; (2) instrument GRC JIT to emit session_id; (3) run every 5 min; (4) SOAR auto-terminates session on hit. Indexes required: `index=grc`, `index=pam`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=beyondtrust:command`, `sourcetype=cyberark:command`, `sourcetype=jit:approval`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: PAM session keystroke/command logs, GRC JIT ticketing events, high-risk command catalogue (high_risk_commands.csv)..
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Curate high_risk_commands.csv with regex patterns; (2) instrument GRC JIT to emit session_id; (3) run every 5 min; (4) SOAR auto-terminates session on hit.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=pam sourcetype=cyberark:command OR sourcetype=beyondtrust:command earliest=-5m
| lookup high_risk_commands.csv command_pattern AS command OUTPUT risk_class
| where risk_class IN ("destructive","mass-data","security-control")
| join session_id [search index=grc sourcetype=jit:approval earliest=-1h | fields session_id ticket_id approver approved_at]
| where isnull(ticket_id)
| table _time session_id user command risk_class target_host
```

Understanding this SPL

**High-risk privileged-session command without JIT approval** — Makes 'we require JIT approval for dangerous commands' an observable, always-on control.

Documented **Data sources**: PAM session keystroke/command logs, GRC JIT ticketing events, high-risk command catalogue (high_risk_commands.csv). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: pam; **sourcetype**: cyberark:command, beyondtrust:command. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=pam, sourcetype=cyberark:command, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where risk_class IN ("destructive","mass-data","security-control")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
• Filters the current rows with `where isnull(ticket_id)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: _time, command, risk_class, session_id, target_host, user.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare results with the audit register and the GRC system so evidence lines up with control owners, attestations, and retention policy.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table of unapproved high-risk commands, bar chart of commands by risk class, single-value 'high-risk commands approved (7d)'.

## SPL

```spl
index=pam sourcetype=cyberark:command OR sourcetype=beyondtrust:command earliest=-5m
| lookup high_risk_commands.csv command_pattern AS command OUTPUT risk_class
| where risk_class IN ("destructive","mass-data","security-control")
| join session_id [search index=grc sourcetype=jit:approval earliest=-1h | fields session_id ticket_id approver approved_at]
| where isnull(ticket_id)
| table _time session_id user command risk_class target_host
```

## Visualization

Table of unapproved high-risk commands, bar chart of commands by risk class, single-value 'high-risk commands approved (7d)'.
